package main import ( "bytes" "fmt" "html" "mime" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs an HTML-formatted email message for user feedback func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Escape user inputs to prevent HTML injection and ensure valid HTML escUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) escSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escContent := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Create structured HTML body with user feedback information htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` User Feedback Submission

New User Feedback Received

Username: %s
Subject: %s
Feedback:
%s
`, escUserName, escSubject, escContent) // Encode subject to handle non-ASCII characters encodedSubject := mime.QEncoding.Encode("UTF-8", subject) // Construct complete email message with required SMTP headers var message bytes.Buffer fmt.Fprintf(&message, "From: %s\r\n", fromAddr) fmt.Fprintf(&message, "To: %s\r\n", toAddr) fmt.Fprintf(&message, "Subject: %s\r\n", encodedSubject) fmt.Fprint(&message, "MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") fmt.Fprint(&message, "Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8\r\n") fmt.Fprint(&message, "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit\r\n") fmt.Fprint(&message, "\r\n") // Separate headers from body with blank line message.Write([]byte(htmlBody)) return message.String(), nil } func main() { // Example usage of BuildFeedbackEmail from := "user@example.com" to := "support@company.com" userName := "Alice Smith" subject := "Payment Processing Issue" feedbackContent := "Hello Support Team,\n\nI attempted to make a payment yesterday but received an error message after entering my card details. The error said 'Transaction failed due to unknown reason'. I've tried multiple times with the same result.\n\nCould you please investigate this issue for me?\n\nThank you,\nAlice Smith" emailMessage, err := BuildFeedbackEmail(from, to, userName, subject, feedbackContent) if err != nil { fmt.Printf("Error building feedback email: %v\n", err) return } fmt.Println("Generated Feedback Email:") fmt.Println("------------------------") fmt.Println(emailMessage) }

package main import ( "fmt" "html" "log" "mime" "net/smtp" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs a complete HTML-formatted email message including headers // that can be sent via net/smtp.SendMail. It handles non-ASCII subject encoding and HTML escaping // of user inputs to prevent injection attacks. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (emailBody string, err error) { // Validate required parameters are non-empty if fromAddr == "" || toAddr == "" || userName == "" || subject == "" || feedbackContent == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("all parameters (fromAddr, toAddr, userName, subject, feedbackContent) must be non-empty") } // Encode subject to support non-ASCII characters (RFC 2047 compliant) encodedSubject := mime.QEncoding.Encode("UTF-8", subject) // Escape user inputs to prevent HTML injection in the email body escapedUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) escapedSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) escapedFeedback := html.EscapeString(feedbackContent) // Construct HTML body with structured feedback information htmlBody := fmt.Sprintf(` User Feedback: %s

New User Feedback Received

Hello Support Team,

You have received new feedback from a user:

User Name: %s
Feedback Subject: %s
Feedback Content: %s

Best regards,

The Feedback System

`, escapedSubject, escapedUserName, escapedSubject, escapedFeedback) // Combine headers and body into a complete RFC 822 compliant email message fullEmail := fmt.Sprintf( "From: %s\r\n"+ "To: %s\r\n"+ "Subject: %s\r\n"+ "MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n"+ "Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"\r\n"+ "\r\n"+ "%s", fromAddr, toAddr, encodedSubject, htmlBody) return fullEmail, nil } func main() { // -------------------------- // Configuration - Update these values with your actual SMTP settings // -------------------------- smtpServer := "smtp.example.com" // e.g., "smtp.gmail.com" for Gmail, "smtp.office365.com" for Outlook smtpPort := "587" // Typical TLS port for SMTP smtpUsername := "your-email@example.com" smtpPassword := "your-password-or-app-password" // Use app password if 2FA is enabled for Gmail // Feedback details (would typically come from user input in a real system) fromAddr := "user@example.com" toAddr := "support@example.com" userName := "John Doe" subject := "Issue with Account Login" feedbackContent := `Hi Support Team, I'm having trouble logging into my account. When I enter my password, I get an error message saying "Invalid credentials". I've tried resetting my password three times using the "Forgot Password" link, but the issue persists. Could you please look into this for me? Thanks, John Doe` // Build the complete email message emailMsg, err := BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr, toAddr, userName, subject, feedbackContent) if err != nil { log.Fatalf("Failed to build email: %v", err) } // Set up SMTP authentication auth := smtp.PlainAuth("", smtpUsername, smtpPassword, smtpServer) // Send the email using net/smtp err = smtp.SendMail( fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", smtpServer, smtpPort), auth, fromAddr, []string{toAddr}, []byte(emailMsg), ) if err != nil { log.Fatalf("Failed to send email: %v", err) } fmt.Println("Feedback email sent successfully to support team!") }
